links.summary: Microsoft's Fairwater AI superfactory is being built around NVIDIA's Blackwell architecture, deploying over 100,000 Blackwell Ultra GPUs in NVIDIA GB300 NVL72 systems globally for inference workloads. Azure's Q4 FY2025 revenue surged 39% year-over-year, with capacity constraints attributed to the insatiable demand for NVIDIA GPU-powered virtual machines. The partnership also extends into software, with NVIDIA Nemotron models integrated into Microsoft SQL Server 2025 and NVIDIA Spectrum-X Ethernet switches deployed in Microsoft's AI superfactories.
sources: Microsoft Azure GTC 2025 Announcement — Azure ND GB200 V6 Series Launch (nvidia.com / microsoft.com, Mar 2025)
NVIDIA Q4 FY2025 Earnings Release — Record $35.6B data center revenue (nvidia.com, Feb 26, 2025)
crn.com — Microsoft Azure Revenue +39% Q4 FY2025 (Jul 2025)

links.summary: Google Cloud remains one of NVIDIA's largest hyperscaler customers, offering H100/B200 clusters to third-party developers via its Vertex AI platform, even as it simultaneously develops competing custom hardware. Google's Ironwood TPU v7, unveiled in 2025, projects 4,614 TFLOPS FP8 — directly competitive with NVIDIA's B200 — and Anthropic signed a deal for up to one million Trillium (TPU v6e) units by 2027, signaling a genuine erosion of NVIDIA's near-monopoly in the inference segment. The AI accelerator market is transitioning from a single-vendor dominant model to a 'two-platform market,' per industry analysts.
sources: lunabase.ai — Google TPU v7 Ironwood vs NVIDIA B200 Performance Analysis (2025)
Seeking Alpha — NVIDIA vs. Google TPU: Is the Monopoly Cracking? (2025)
Reuters — Anthropic Signs Deal for 1M Google TPUs by 2027 (Nov 2025)

links.summary: AWS announced general availability of Amazon EC2 P6-B200 instances in January 2026, featuring NVIDIA Blackwell B200 GPUs promising double the ML/HPC performance over prior generation hardware. At AWS re:Invent 2025, the two companies revealed a multigenerational collaboration including NVLink Fusion integration into AWS custom silicon (Trainium4 and Graviton CPUs) and inclusion of NVIDIA Nemotron open models in Amazon Bedrock. Amazon committed $200 billion in AI infrastructure capex for 2026, with NVIDIA GPU procurement representing a core line item.
sources: NVIDIA Press Release — NVIDIA and AWS Expand Partnership at re:Invent 2025 (nvidia.com, Dec 2025)
ExtremeTech — Amazon EC2 P6-B200 Instances GA (Jan 2026)
Amazon About Page — AWS re:Invent 2025 AI Infrastructure Highlights (aboutamazon.com, Dec 2025)

links.summary: Meta is NVIDIA's largest social-media-sector customer for Blackwell GPUs, deploying massive clusters for training Llama 4 and its next-generation recommendation engines. In early 2026, Meta's 'Llama 4 Scout' and 'Llama 4 Maverick' models were publicly confirmed to have been trained on NVIDIA infrastructure. Meta also signed a multi-billion dollar deal in March 2026 to rent Google TPUs, framing its strategy as hardware-agnostic — but NVIDIA remains the dominant substrate for Meta's primary training runs.
sources: Meta LlamaCon 2025 — Llama 4 Architecture Reveal and Training Infrastructure (Apr 2025)
NVIDIA GTC 2025 — Meta Partnership Announcement (Mar 2025)
Digitimes — Meta Explores TPUs to Reduce NVIDIA Dependence (Dec 2025)

links.summary: Microsoft Azure and Google Cloud are the two fastest-growing hyperscalers in Q4 2025, competing intensely for enterprise AI contracts. Azure's AI business surpassed $13 billion in annual recurring revenue as of January 2025 (175% YoY growth), while Google Cloud posted 48% revenue growth in Q4 2025 — the fastest among the Big Three — driven by Gemini 3 and its Vertex AI platform. Microsoft holds a decisive advantage in enterprise seat penetration via Microsoft 365 and Teams copilot integrations, while Google leads in foundational AI research and proprietary TPU infrastructure.
sources: IoT Analytics / eWeek — Cloud AI Engagement Market Share Report Q4 2025 (cloudcomputing-news.net, 2025)
Quantumrun — Azure AI ARR Hits $13B (Jan 2025)
CRN — Google Cloud Q4 2025 Revenue +48% YoY (Jan 2026)

links.summary: AWS and Azure are the world's two largest cloud platforms, fighting for the same enterprise workloads. As of Q4 2025, AWS held 28% global cloud market share vs. Azure's 21%, but Azure grew at 31% YoY in Microsoft's Q2 FY2026 vs. AWS's 20% in Q4 2025. The rivalry is increasingly centered on AI: AWS pushes Amazon Bedrock with Anthropic's Claude, while Azure leans on its exclusive OpenAI partnership and deeper Microsoft 365 integration as its primary enterprise differentiation. Both are spending hundreds of billions in data center capex to capture the $800B+ cloud market.
sources: Statista / Holori — Global Cloud Market Share Q4 2025 (2025)
programming-helper.com — Azure vs AWS Growth Rates FY2026 Q2 (2026)
CTO Magazine — AI Cloud Competition: Azure, AWS, and the Enterprise Race (2025)

links.summary: Microsoft and Meta have maintained a multi-year open-source AI distribution partnership centered on the Llama model family. Azure AI Foundry integrated Llama 4 Scout and Llama 4 Maverick on April 5, 2025 — models handling up to 10 million tokens with multimodal capabilities — making them available to Azure enterprise customers alongside xAI's Grok and Anthropic's Claude. This partnership allows Meta to reach enterprise developers via Azure's distribution while Microsoft broadens its model catalog beyond its exclusive OpenAI relationship.
sources: Microsoft Azure Blog — Llama 4 Scout and Maverick Now Available in Azure AI Foundry (microsoft.com, Apr 5, 2025)
Redmond Magazine — Microsoft Expands AI Model Marketplace with Meta Llama 4 (Apr 2025)
Streamlinefeed — Azure AI Marketplace Adds Llama 4 and Grok (May 2025)

links.summary: Google Cloud and AWS are locked in an intense rivalry for the generative-AI enterprise market. In Q4 2025, Google Cloud's $17.7B revenue grew 48% YoY (the fastest among hyperscalers), while AWS's $35.6B grew 24%, its fastest in 13 quarters. Alphabet committed $175–185B in 2026 capex (double 2025) to expand data centers and challenge AWS's capacity lead, and revenue from GCP products built on Gemini surged nearly 400% YoY in Q4 2025. The two also compete in AI-enhanced consumer hardware (Google Assistant vs. Alexa) and enterprise foundation model platforms (Vertex AI vs. Amazon Bedrock).
sources: CRN — Q4 2025 Cloud Market: AWS 24% Growth, Google Cloud 48% Growth (Jan 2026)
The Smart Investor — Alphabet $175B Capex Plan for 2026 AI Infrastructure (thesmartinvestor.com.sg, Feb 2026)
Medium — AWS Bedrock vs Google Vertex AI: The Enterprise AI Platform Race (2025)

links.summary: Google and Meta compete as the world's two largest AI-driven digital advertising platforms — Google generating $237B in 2024 ad revenue versus Meta's $201B in 2025 — while simultaneously entering a surprising infrastructure partnership. In March 2026, Meta signed a multi-billion dollar, multi-year deal to rent Google TPUs for AI model development, acknowledging the superior cost economics of Google's Trillium chips for inference. On the LLM frontier, Meta's open-source Llama models compete with Google's Gemma family, though Meta's reported pivot toward closed-source development in Q1 2026 may shift this dynamic.
sources: Digitimes — Meta Signs Multi-Billion Dollar Google TPU Deal (Mar 2026)
TheNews.com.pk — Meta to Rent Google TPUs in Multi-Year Agreement (Mar 2026)
Marketing Dive — Meta Advertising Revenue and AI Automation Roadmap (2025)

links.summary: Meta and AWS have a well-established model distribution partnership, with Llama 4 Scout 17B and Llama 4 Maverick 17B made available on Amazon Bedrock and SageMaker JumpStart from April 28, 2025. AWS and Meta jointly launched a startup accelerator program in 2025 offering $200,000 in AWS credits to companies building with Llama models. Meta also uses AWS for secondary cloud capacity, and Amazon's custom Trainium and Inferentia chips are positioned as synergistic workload partners for Llama inference deployments, though Meta is actively diversifying its cloud footprint.
sources: About Amazon — Meta Llama 4 Now Available in Amazon Bedrock (aboutamazon.com, Apr 28, 2025)
Amazon AWS Blog — Llama 4 Scout and Maverick on SageMaker JumpStart (amazon.com, Apr 2025)
PYMNTS / aicerts.ai — AWS-Meta Startup Accelerator Program Launch (Aug 2025)
